intent: Analyse a single-cell ATAC-seq dataset. Use a small PUBLIC scATAC-seq dataset (e.g. 10x's PBMC scATAC, or a snapATAC2/episcanpy built-in). Do basic QC (TSS enrichment, fragment/feature counts), build the cell × peak (or bin) matrix, run TF-IDF/LSI, cluster, and show a UMAP coloured by cluster.

blocks.text: I'll help you analyze a single-cell ATAC-seq dataset. Let me start by understanding what's available and planning the workflow.

input.query: single-cell ATAC-seq workflow snapATAC best practice

blocks.text: Let me search more broadly for ATAC-seq and epigenomic tools:
Good — I'll use **snapATAC2** (via Python), which is the standard modern single-cell ATAC-seq workflow. Let me ensure it's available and set up the analysis:
Perfect. Now let me plan the workflow:

input.title: Single-cell ATAC-seq Analysis (snapATAC2)
input.summary: QC → feature matrix → TF-IDF/LSI embedding → clustering → UMAP visualization
input.rationale: Demonstrates the standard modern snapATAC2 workflow on public scATAC data, from QC metrics through unsupervised discovery
input.assumptions:
<item>Using snapATAC2 built-in demo dataset (10x PBMC scATAC-seq) — no external download needed</item>
  <item>Analyzing as a single integrated dataset (no batch correction)</item>
  <item>QC filtering based on standard TSS enrichment (≥2) and fragment count thresholds</item>
  <item>Using peak regions for the feature matrix (vs bins)</item>
  <item>TF-IDF + SVD for dimensionality reduction, then Leiden clustering</item>
  <item>Outputs: QC plots, feature matrix, embedding, clusters, final UMAP</item>
</assumptions>
<parameter name="steps">
  <item>
    <title>Load snapATAC2 demo data (10x PBMC scATAC)</title>
    <description>Fetch the built-in example fragment file and metadata</description>
    <expected_outputs>
      <item>AnnData-like object with fragments, dimensions ~10k cells</item>
    </expected_outputs>
  </item>
  <item>
    <title>QC: Compute TSS enrichment & fragment counts</title>
    <description>Calculate TSS-enrichment score and per-cell fragment count; visualize distributions</description>
    <expected_outputs>
      <item>TSS enrichment histogram</item>
      <item>Fragment count histogram</item>
      <item>Filtered cell count</item>
    </expected_outputs>
  </item>
  <item>
    <title>Build cell × peak feature matrix</title>
    <description>Call peaks (or use reference peaks) and count overlaps per cell</description>
    <expected_outputs>
      <item>Feature matrix (cells × peaks)</item>
      <item>Peak annotation</item>
    </expected_outputs>
  </item>
  <item>
    <title>Normalize & reduce: TF-IDF + LSI (SVD)</title>
    <description>Apply term-frequency / inverse-document-frequency normalization, then spectral / latent semantic indexing for dimensionality reduction</description>
    <expected_outputs>
      <item>Normalized counts</item>
      <item>LSI embedding (top PCs)</item>
    </expected_outputs>
  </item>
  <item>
    <title>Leiden clustering on LSI</title>
    <description>Graph-based clustering in the LSI space</description>
    <expected_outputs>
      <item>Cluster assignment per cell</item>
    </expected_outputs>
  </item>
  <item>
    <title>UMAP from LSI, colored by cluster</title>
    <description>Non-linear layout for visualization</description>
    <expected_outputs>
      <item>UMAP plot with cluster colors</item>
    </expected_outputs>
  </item>
